# PNA Spec

> Universal specification for personal network applications (PNAs). Defines vocabulary, goals, use cases, axes, composition (how PNAs get built), architectural commitments, and slot contracts. Targeted at AI agents and humans building PNAs together.

**New here? Start with the skill.** If you are an AI agent that landed here cold, read [`pna-toolkit/SKILL.md`](pna-toolkit/SKILL.md) first. It is the entry point: it routes you into the spec, contracts, and reference designs for whichever flow you need — **evaluate** ("is this app safe to install?"), **build**, or **contribute**.

## Skill (agent entry point)

- [pna-toolkit/SKILL.md](pna-toolkit/SKILL.md) — canonical PNA Toolkit skill for AI agents. Three flows: build a conformant PNA from the spec, evaluate a candidate PNA's conformance against the spec, contribute a design back to the toolkit
- [docs/users-guide.md](docs/users-guide.md) — human-facing step-by-step instructions for each flow
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) — how reference designs (and any spec changes they motivate) get accepted into the toolkit

## Spec

- [PNA Spec](spec/PNA_Spec.md) — universal spec; Toolkit-Version 0.1 (draft)
- [Axes](spec/axes.md) — the Axes a PNA varies along, attested picks per Axis, and the `RZ-*` realizations + constraints each pick brings (the conditional ACs they entail live in PNA_Spec.md)
- [Use cases](spec/use_cases.md) — attested classes of PNA (Minimum Viable PNA, Directory Archive, Personal Relationship Manager [draft], Multi-PNA ecosystem [target v0.2+])
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md) — toolkit version history

## Typed contracts

- [contracts/](contracts/) — JSON Schema, OpenAPI fragments, SQL DDL, and TypeScript declarations for the load-bearing interfaces (worker handshake, distribution auth, comms transport, shared/private DB schemas, five canonical MCP servers — Shared Data Ops, Private Data Ops, Ingestion, Communications, Diagnostics)

## Reference designs

- [reference_designs/](reference_designs/) — accepted reference designs (each a working PNA demonstrating one valid combination of axis picks against the spec). Index at `reference_designs/README.md`; per-design records and Architecture documents live in subdirectories.
